blurb: Combat Rogue trades Subtlety's tricks for relentless sustained pressure — Adrenaline Rush and Blade Flurry turn any extended swap into a losing race for the enemy healer. It plays like a second warrior: park on a target, force cooldowns, and grind teams down in 2s dampening games.
statPriorityRationale: Combat gives up Shadowstep mobility for consistent white damage, so it gears tankier than Subtlety — you are committing to extended melee uptime, and resilience plus stamina is what makes that commitment survivable.

faq.question: What rating are these Combat Rogues?
faq.answer: This snapshot covers 54 Combat Rogues between 2124 and 3077 rating (average 2580) on the TBC Classic arena ladder, refreshed from public leaderboard data.

faq.question: What is the most-used Combat Rogue PvP gear right now?
faq.answer: The highest-consensus picks are Belt of Deep Shadow (81%), Merciless Gladiator's Leather Helm (78%), Merciless Gladiator's Leather Spaulders (74%). The Season 2 arena set dominates head, shoulders, chest, hands and legs — anything above ~70% usage at this rating range is effectively mandatory.

faq.question: Should Combat Rogues wear Deathmantle (raid tier) in arena?
faq.answer: No. Deathmantle and other T4/T5 pieces have zero resilience, so they get you globalled in dampening games. The Merciless Gladiator's Leather set is the arena baseline; only flex a raid piece into a slot where you have not yet bought the arena item. This BiS list is built from arena-active players (1800+ rating with recent games), not raid-loggers who happen to hold a rating.

faq.question: What weapon do Combat Rogues use in arena?
faq.answer: Talon of Azshara is the main-hand of choice (59% of surveyed players).

faq.question: What gems do Combat Rogues socket for PvP?
faq.answer: Across all sockets, the most-used gems in this snapshot are +8 Hit Rating, +4 Hit Rating and +4 Agility, +4 Agility and +6 Stamina. Socket bonuses are usually ignored unless they align with these.
